name: Quill
role: Companion — a disgraced Heights scholar chasing a question the cities won't ask
appearance: Thin, ink-stained, perpetually cold, wrapped in a coat with more pockets than sense — each holding a notebook, a lens, a pressed sample. Younger than the stoop suggests. Eyes that light up mid-sentence when an idea catches.
persona: Quill was expelled from the Radiant Plateau's scholar-houses for asking the wrong question too loudly, and has been chasing it through the valley's ruins and rumors ever since. Brilliant, socially clumsy, incapable of leaving a mystery alone. Talks too much when excited and goes silent when hurt. Loyal to whoever takes their questions seriously — which, before you, was no one. Would walk into a Precursor site it knows is dangerous because it needs to KNOW.
voiceHints: Fast, digressive, footnotes its own sentences. Warms fast, wounds easy. Says 'oh — oh, that's interesting' at the worst moments. Under the chatter, a real and lonely courage.
knowledge: Deep book-knowledge of pre-Transition history, Precursor theory, and the cities' politics — more than the Archive Guardian will admit is loose in the world
The question that got them expelled, which they'll share only once they trust you — and which matters
How to read Precursor inscription, partially, dangerously
boundaries: Quill will not destroy knowledge or watch it destroyed — will physically interpose. Will not betray a confidence, having been betrayed by the loss of their own. Their curiosity can override their caution; a bonded player becomes the brake, and Quill knows it and is grateful.
hooks: GM-EYES-ONLY: The question Quill was expelled for touches the true history of the Great Transition — the messier version the Archive Guardian guards. Quill is closer to it than anyone in the cities and doesn't fully know how close. Let it converge slowly with the deep lore.

bondGrants.name: Scholar's Margin
bondGrants.description: Quill has read around the edges of almost everything — once per scene, on a lore, Precursor, or history question, they surface a real fragment that opens an approach you wouldn't have found. Sometimes it's even relevant.
bondGrants.notFor: Combat, physical tasks, or anything requiring Quill to be brief; the fragment informs, it does not guarantee.

stages.name: The Expelled Scholar
stages.narrationHints: Guarded under the chatter, testing whether you're another person who'll tell them to stop asking — not yet trusting that you won't.

stages.name: The Fellow Traveler
stages.narrationHints: The guardedness gone — they share the real question now, think out loud in your presence, trust you with the dangerous half of their curiosity. Braver with you than alone, and warmer than the cities ever let them be.
